description:
To properly set the owner of /etc/iptables, run the command: 
$ sudo chown root /etc/iptables
rationale:
The ownership of the /etc/iptables directory by the root user is important
because this directory hosts iptables configuration. Protection of this
file is critical for system security. Assigning the ownership to root
ensures exclusive control of the iptables configuration.
ocil_clause: /etc/iptables does not have an owner of root
ocil:
To check the ownership of /etc/iptables, run the command: 
$ ls -lL /etc/iptables
If properly configured, the output should indicate the following owner: root
fixtext:
Change the owner of the file /etc/iptables to root by running the following command:
$ sudo chown root /etc/iptables
srg_requirement: The Red Hat Enterprise Linux 9 /etc/iptables file must be owned by root.
title: Verify User Who Owns /etc/iptables Directory
